articles.title: Thunderstorm warning in 5 Himachal districts: Clear weather likely from May 18, Una hits 36.2°C - Bhaskar English
articles.search_term: heatwave (Bilaspur OR Chamba OR Hamirpur OR Kangra OR Kinnaur OR Kullu OR "Lahaul and Spiti" OR Mandi OR Shimla OR "Sirmaur (Sirmour)" OR Solan OR Una) Himachal Pradesh
articles.full_text:
Thunderstorm warning in 5 Himachal districts:Clear weather likely from May 18, Una hits 36.2°C
Himachal Pradesh is likely to experience rain over the next two days, including today and tomorrow. The Meteorological Department has issued a yellow alert for thunderstorms and strong winds in Chamba, Kangra, Kullu, Mandi, and Shimla districts. During this period, winds may blow at speeds of up to 40 km per hour.
Light rain expected in higher hills on May 17
On May 17, light drizzle is expected in the higher reaches of the state, while the lower regions are likely to remain mostly dry. From May 18 onward, the weather is expected to turn clear across the state, leading to a rise in temperatures by four to five degrees and warmer conditions.
Temperatures rise above normal across key cities
Temperatures in 10 cities have already crossed 30°C. Una recorded the highest at 36.2°C. Dharamshala touched 33.0°C, which is 2.5 degrees above normal. Mandi recorded 34.4°C (2.2 degrees above normal), while Bhuntar reached 33.0°C, 2.1 degrees above normal.
19% excess rainfall recorded in May so far
The state has received 19% more rainfall than normal so far in May. Several districts have seen significantly higher rainfall, including Bilaspur (100%), Hamirpur (105%), Kangra (127%), Kullu (30%), Mandi (80%), Shimla (106%), Sirmaur (128%), Solan (15%), and Una (194%).
However, Chamba, Kinnaur, and Lahaul-Spiti are among the districts where rainfall has remained below normal levels.
